judgment.summary: Strong earnings growth and cash flow are offset by a high trailing P/E and concerns about AI displacement.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 6.4%; next earnings date is 2026-05-21.
positives: Quarterly earnings growth 54.3% YoY — demonstrating strong profitability improvements.
Free cash flow $3.01B — robust cash generation supports operations and investment.
Forward P/E 10.17x — significantly lower than trailing P/E, suggesting future earnings growth is priced in.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $178.16, indicating positive sentiment from the sell-side.
negatives: Trailing P/E 48.88x — valuation appears stretched based on historical earnings.
Operating margin 12.0% and Profit margin 7.3% — could be improved relative to revenue growth.
Debt/Equity 48.96 — while not excessive, it's a notable leverage level.
Recent negative commentary from Jim Cramer and a downgrade highlight potential market concerns.

news.paragraph: Workday is facing headwinds from prominent figures like Jim Cramer, who suggests AI may displace its services, and a recent downgrade from BWG Global. These developments cast a shadow over the stock ahead of its upcoming earnings report. Traders will be watching the earnings call for management's response to these concerns and any updated outlook on AI's impact.

headlines.title: Jim Cramer Says Workday Has Become a “Poster Boy” For AI Displacement

examples.body: DOCU : Same pattern as observed in the chart of $WDAY
$WDAY : Should break to the upside of the base that has formed
$WDAY tomorrow’s chop is on the way
$NOW yesterday there was a stampede into the stock before the evil hedge funds flipped the quant switch at 10am. Now it’s cheaper. So where are those stampedin…
